D2.name: Task Management & Decomposition
gaps: No knowledge atoms available for this domain

key_techniques.summary: Sliding Window with Overlap: Fixed-size context windows that slide through conversation/code with overlap to maintain continuity.
Context Provenance Tracking: Maintaining source attribution for all context, enabling debugging, trust evaluation, and context poisoning detection.
Semantic Chunking: Code-aware chunking that preserves semantic boundaries (functions, classes, modules) rather than arbitrary token limits.
Context Caching: Caching frequently-used or expensive-to-compute context to reduce latency and API costs across multiple queries.
Task-Conditioned Context: Context selection and filtering conditioned on current task type and phase, retrieving different context for coding vs.

failure_modes.what_breaks: Naive Truncation: Truncating context without any prioritization or relevance assessment, leading to loss of critical information and degraded model performance.
failure_modes.how_to_detect: Monitor for wasted tokens, model confusion, or degraded performance

failure_modes.what_breaks: Context Stuffing: Maximally filling context windows with all available information without prioritization or filtering. Results in 23-45% of tokens wasted on irrelevant content, "lost in the middle" phenomenon, increased API costs, and model confusion from contradictory or redundant context.
failure_modes.how_to_detect: Monitor for wasted tokens, model confusion, or degraded performance

D5.name: Code Intelligence & Representations

key_techniques.summary: Test-First Pattern (TDD): Write tests before implementation code.
Test Pyramid Pattern: Structure tests in a pyramid shape with many unit tests at the bottom, fewer integration tests in the middle, and few E2E tests at the top.
Multi-Stage Validation Pattern: Progress tests through multiple validation stages: local â†’ PR â†’ integration â†’ staging â†’ canary.
Happy Path / Sad Path Pattern: Explicitly test both success scenarios (happy path) and error scenarios (sad path).
Test Double Pattern: Use various types of test doubles (dummy, stub, spy, mock, fake) to replace real dependencies appropriately.
